steps.title: Identify the secrecy problem
steps.content: The protocol is meant to ensure compensation for Indigenous communities, but it allows corporations to keep research agreements confidential.

steps.title: Reject effects secrecy does not imply
steps.content:
• Choice A: The concern is not that corporations will earn less money.
  • Choice B: Confidentiality does not limit how much research corporations can conduct.
  • Choice D: The passage does not discuss Indigenous communities learning harvesting methods from corporations.

steps.title: Connect secrecy to oversight
steps.content: If agreements remain confidential, outside observers cannot verify whether the communities are receiving fair compensation. The answer is C.
